MINI MINUTES:

Hearing RE: DEFENDANT LEONARD GRAY, M.D., LEONARD W. GRAY, M.D., A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendants maintained their initial burden of production and show that defendants did not breach the standard of care, nor did they cause injury to decedent. (Madden Decl., Ex. D [Parrett Decl., pars. 1-15].) Indeed, plaintiff admits that defendants shifted the burden. (Opposition 4:1-10.)

Order signed.

DEFENDANT LEONARD GRAY, M.D., LEONARD W. GRAY, M.D., A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING PRODUCTION OF AUTOPSY PHOTOS AND SLIDES (PART 1 OF 2)
Off Calendar Jan-31-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Production Of Autopsy Photos And Slides
Off Calendar Jan-31-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANTS LEONARD GRAY, M.D., LEONARD W. GRAY, M.D., A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION's Motion For An Order Compelling Production Of Autopsy Photos And Slides.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. This is a professional negligence action in the form of a wrongful death suit against the defendant physician, Leonard Gray, M.D. It arises out of plastic surgery services provided by the defendant to the decedent Eva Marie Derdak. The defendant physician has retained the services of a forensic pathologist to review the treatment he provided to the decedent. This defense expert has expressed the need to review photographs taken and histological slides prepared by the Alameda County Coroner's Office as part of the autopsy performed on decedent. The Coroner's representatives and counsel point to Code of Civil Procedure 129 which prohibits production of such autopsy materials to a third party such as the defendant absent court order issued on a showing of good cause. The defendant has filed a motion to secure a court order pursuant to Section 129 in order to secure the autopsy-related materials that his defense expert states are needed to make an accurate determination of the cause of the decedent's death: opioid overdose (the Coroner's conclusion) or as a result of medical negligence by the defendant. Plaintiffs have not filed any opposition to the motion to secure the necessary court order. Good cause appearing, IT IS HEREBY ORDERED that the Motion for an Order Compelling Production of Autopsy Photos and Slides by the Alameda County Coroner's Office is GRANTED. Counsel for defendant as the moving party shall prepare a proposed order in compliance with Rule 3.1312 of the Rules of Court. In that proposed order, attention should be directed to the specificity required by the Coroner's Office with respect to the autopsy materials to be copied and produced. Costs of production shall be paid by defendant, which shall include an additional copy of the autopsy materials for plaintiffs, if requested.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)
